data.title: Stock index futures gain after Trump exempts tech from reciprocal tariffs
data.description:
Stock index futures rode high on Monday, as President Donald Trump announced temporary tariff exemptions on smartphones, computers, and other electronics primarily imported from China.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +1.5%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +1.9%, and Dow futures (INDU) +1.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 6 basis points to 4.43%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 4 basis points to 3.92%.

Trump's administration on Friday exempted smartphones, computers, and other electronics mostly imported from China from reciprocal tariffs, providing major relief for American tech players.

Wall Street on Friday overcame a volatile morning trading session to push higher, ending a wild week on a positive note. Tariffs dominated headlines, with the latest being that the Trump administration had made “very good progress” on trade negotiations.

"Tariffs continued to dominate headlines over the weekend, starting with news late on Friday that smartphones, computers, and some other electronics would be exempt from U.S. reciprocal tariffs," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

Turning to the week ahead, while tariffs will remain the center of attention, investors will also be keeping an eye on incoming U.S. data with the March retail sales and industrial production prints on Wednesday, Sidorov added.

"Over the weekend, Trump partially retreated from the retreat, emphasizing that a 20% tax would apply and additional unspecified taxes on technology are coming. This erratic policy making leads to investors questioning the existence of a competent plan," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

The week ahead will also see a slew of big companies reporting their earnings, including Bank of America (BAC), Netflix (NFLX), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and American Express (AXP).

The economic calendar is on the lighter side today, with mainly the Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic and Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin's speeches on tap.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Q1 strong earnings beat on gains in FICC, equities revenue (updated)
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) Q1 earnings and revenue significantly outperformed Wall Street consensus estimates, reflecting robust increases in the bank's fixed income, currency and commodities revenue as well as equity revenue. A lower provision for credit losses also helped.

Q1 GAAP EPS of $14.12, topping the average analyst estimate of $12.27, rose from $11.95 in Q4 2024 and $11.58 in Q1 2024.

Total revenue of $15.06B, beating the $14.7B consensus, increased from $13.9B in the prior quarter and $14.2B a year ago.

Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) stock rose 3.6% in Monday premarket trading. (Updated at 9:00 AM ET).

"The beat largely reflected better-than-expected equities sales and trading revenue and a lower than expected effective tax rate (adjusted pre-tax income was 4% above our estimate), partly offset by lower-than-forecast investment banking fees," said HSBC analyst Saul Martinez. (Added at 9:00 AM ET.)

Q1 net interest income of $2.90B, missing the Visible Alpha consensus of $3.30B, climbed from $2.35B in Q4 and $1.37B in last year's Q1.

Provision for credit losses of $287M, smaller than the Visible Alpha consensus of $412M, dropped from $351M in the previous quarter and $318M a year ago.

Total operating expenses of $9.13B rose from $8.26 B in Q4 and $8.66B in last year's Q1.

Annualized return on average common shareholders' equity was 16.9%, up 2.3 percentage points from Q4 2024 and 2.1 pp from Q1 2024.

Loans of $210B at March 31, 2025, grew from $196B at Dec. 31, 2024. Deposits of $471B increased from $433B at the end of Q4 2024.

Headcount totaled edged up to 46,600 at the end of Q1, compared with 46,500 at the end of Q4.

Global Banking & Market net revenue of $10.7B climbed 26% Q/Q and 10% Y/Y. Investment banking fees of $1.91B dipped 7% Q/Q and 8% Y/Y, reflecting lower net revenues in advisory compared with a strong prior year period, partly offset by higher net revenues in debt underwriting, primarily driven by asset-backed and investment-grade activity. FICC net revenue of $4.40B surged 61% Q/Q and increased 2% Y/Y. Equity revenue of $4.19B jumped 21% Q/Q and 27% Y/Y, primarily from significantly higher net revenues in equities intermediation (primarily reflecting significantly higher net revenues in derivatives) and in equities financing (primarily reflecting significantly higher net revenues in portfolio financing). 

Asset & Wealth Management net revenue of $3.68B dropped 22% Q/Q and 3% Y/Y; management and other fees totaled $2.70B, down 4 % Q/Q and up 10% Y/Y.

Platform Solutions net revenue of $676M edged up 1% Q/Q and dipped 3% Y/Y.

"Our strong results this quarter have demonstrated that in times of great uncertainty, clients turn to Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) for execution and insight," said Chairman and CEO David Solomon.

Conference call at 9:30 AM ET.

Earlier, Goldman Sachs GAAP EPS of $14.12 beats by $1.85, revenue of $15.06B beats by $350M

data.title: Amentum secures $247M global counter-threat finance contract
data.description:
• Amentum (NYSE:AMTM) on Monday said it has secured a $247.6 million contract from the U.S. Department of Defense Air Combat Command (ACC), Acquisition Management and Integration Center (AMIC), Counternarcotics and Global Threats (CN&GT) Division.
  • Under this contract, Amentum will deliver solutions to the DOD in the counter-transnational organized crime, counter-drug, and counter-terrorism missions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cricut downgraded at Citi as Citi prefers AI networking stocks
data.description:
• Cricut (NASDAQ:CRCT) was downgraded to Sell at Citi on Monday as the investment firm said it prefers artificial intelligence-exposed stocks in the communications equipment and hardware space.
  • “We expect a rally in the group on Trump administration reciprocal tariff exemptions on PCs, smartphones and 20 other products announced over the weekend from the 125% tariff on Chinese imports and 10% baseline tariff on imports for other countries, while the 20% tariff on all Chinese goods remains in effect,” analysts at the firm wrote in a note to clients. "That said, we lower our data center capex & PC models ~5%/2% to reflect macro induced weak demand correlated with Citi’s in-house ~70bps global GDP contraction view in 2025.”
  • In addition to the Cricut downgrade, Citi said its top picks in the communications equipment and hardware space are Cisco (NASDAQ:CSCO), Amphenol (APH) and Coherent (COHR).

data.title: DuPont upgraded at KeyBanc amid revisions in Q1 chemicals preview
data.description:
DuPont de Nemours (NYSE:DD) late Sunday was upgraded to Overweight from a previous investment rating of Sector Weight by analysts at KeyBanc Securities. They pointed to the chemical maker’s strong balance sheet and defensive characteristics amid economic stress.

Despite a 21% stock decline since March 31, DuPont (NYSE:DD) is well-positioned with secular-growth businesses in electronics and water, Aleksey Yefremov, research analyst at KeyBanc, said in an April 13 note to clients.

The recent tariff exemption granted to iPhone maker Apple (AAPL) also helps to de-risk DuPont’s (DD) China exposure, he added. KeyBanc’s new price target for DuPont is $81, based on an estimated enterprise value that’s 12.8 times earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization for 2025, even after trimming ebitda estimates by 5%.

First-quarter chemicals preview

The upgrade of DuPont (DD) comes as KeyBanc provides a preview of what investors should expect during first-quarter earnings season. Chemical companies are under a cloud of economic uncertainty and already are feeling the early drag from new U.S. tariffs, with weak March demand trends likely extending into April, KeyBanc said.

Tariff effects seen ahead of earnings

“We're taking a first crack at reflecting the tariff uncertainty in our estimates,” Yefremov said.

Updates on how tariffs are affecting demand in April and May could be more telling than first-quarter results themselves, he added, especially as companies weigh their exposure to high-tariff countries like China and Europe. The analyst expects most firms to report early signs of demand weakness.

KeyBanc trimmed its 2025 ebitda estimates across its chemicals coverage by 5%, placing the firm 10% below consensus. Volume expectations for industrial, consumer, and electronics products are each reduced by 3%, with global auto production cut by 5%.

Assumptions also include Brent crude (CO1:COM) staying in the $60- to $65-a-barrel range, a scenario that could eventually bring relief to specialty firms reliant on raw material inputs.

Recession not base case, but risks remain

While a recession is not the base case, Yefremov’s downside scenario suggests a 28% median stock drop across the sector if a downturn materializes, with six companies facing potential losses over 40%.

“It's too early to declare a recession case anywhere close to being priced in,” he noted.

Earnings watch: Bright spots, weak links

Positive Standouts: Strong results are expected from Corteva (NYSE:CTVA), FMC (NYSE:FMC) and Celanese (NYSE:CE). Axalta (NYSE:AXTA) and PPG Industries (NYSE:PPG) may also deliver solid first-quarter results, though softness in autos and consumer segments could weigh on the second quarter.

Cautious View: Sherwin-Williams (NYSE:SHW) is expected to struggle in the first half, but Yefremov sees coatings companies benefiting in late 2025 as raw material costs decline.

Turnaround Potential: Huntsman (NYSE:HUN), despite bearish sentiment, could surprise to the upside as MDI margins rise amid tariffs and lower benzene costs.

Challenged Names: Dow (NYSE:DOW), LyondellBasell (NYSE:LYB) and Westlake (NYSE:WLK) face pressure from weaker polyethylene exports to China and softening oil prices. U.S. ethylene and propylene prices are sliding, reflecting deteriorating demand.

Caution on leverage and M&A

Yefremov warned that markets are increasingly intolerant of rising leverage. He advised avoiding large acquisitions and cutting dividends if free cash flow is under pressure.

“The market would punish nearly any company — except perhaps Westlake (NYSE:WLK) — where leverage increases by [a multiple of] 1 time or more due to an acquisition,” he said.

Structural concerns beyond the cycle

Beyond the current slowdown, Yefremov sees deeper issues in the chemicals space.

“The industry has structural challenges that go beyond a typical cycle,” he said. “Our assumptions for trough earnings have been steadily revised lower over the past 24 months.”

Since April 1, investor behavior has followed a classic recession playbook: dumping small caps, high-debt names and those with outsized exposure to China.

“High U.S. exposure is preferred,” according to KeyBanc.

data.title: A-Mark Precious Metals appoints Cary Dickson as CFO
data.description:
• A-Mark Precious Metals (NASDAQ:AMRK) has appointed Cary Dickson as chief financial officer effective July 1, 2025.
  • He will succeed current CFO Kathleen Simpson-Taylor who will retire at the current fiscal year.
  • Dickson had previously served as the company’s CFO from November 2015 through September 2019.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: La Rosa granted 180-day extension by Nasdaq to regain compliance
data.description:
• La Rosa (NASDAQ:LRHC) Monday said that it had received written notification from Nasdaq that the company was eligible for a 180-day extension to regain compliance.
  • La Rosa (NASDAQ:LRHC) now has until October 6 to meet the requirement.

data.title: Giant Tiger partners with DoorDash, Uber Eats to launch on-demand delivery
data.description:
• Giant Tiger Stores has launched on-demand delivery through new national partnerships with DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) and Uber Eats under which it plans to deliver products within an hour,  the company said on Monday.
  • the services will be available across Alberta, Manitoba, New Brunswick, Nova Scotia, Ontario, Prince Edward Island, Quebec, and Saskatchewan.
  • DASH +1.28% premarket to $182.81.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Verve jumps after early-stage trial data for gene editing therapy
data.description:
Verve Therapeutics (NASDAQ:VERV) shares climbed ~23% in the premarket on Monday after the company said its gene editing therapy VERVE-102 led to dose-dependent therapeutic effects among patients with high cholesterol in an early-stage clinical trial.

Citing initial data from its Heart-2 Phase 1b trial, the biotech added that there were no serious adverse events linked to VERVE-102, a single-course base editing medicine targeting the PCSK9 gene, which is involved in regulating LDL cholesterol (LDL-C), also known as bad cholesterol.

Heart-2 enrolled adults with heterozygous familial hypercholesterolemia (HeFH) and/or premature coronary artery disease (CAD). The two patient groups are characterized by high LDL-C levels in the blood.

Citing data from 14 patients who received three different VERVE-102 doses, Verve (NASDAQ:VERV) said that trial subjects well-tolerated the drug with no clinically significant lab anomalies.

As for efficacy, a single treatment course has led to dose-dependent declines in blood PCSK9 protein levels and LDL-C. Specifically, the maximum and mean LDL-C reductions stood at 69% and 53% in four patients who received a 0.6 mg/kg dose of VERVE-102, respectively.

In H2 2025, VERV plans to report final dose escalation data from Heart-2 and begin dosing in its Phase 2b clinical trial for the drug.  

Additionally, Eli Lilly (LLY) is expected to decide whether to join VERV to develop and commercialize the company's PCSK9 program in H2.

data.title: Longevity Health to combine with 20/20 BioLabs in all-stock deal
data.description:
Longevity Health (NASDAQ:XAGE) Monday announced the execution of a merger agreement with 20/20 BioLabs to combine the companies in an all-stock transaction.

Post-closing equity valuation of the combined company is expected to be approximately $99 million, with 20/20 having a pre-money equity valuation at about $50 million.

The combined company is expected to continue to trade on Nasdaq under the symbol “XAGE,” and the deal is anticipated to double Longevity’s fiscal year 2025 anticipated revenue from approximately $3-4 million to approximately $7-8 million.

This merger follows Longevity’s acquisition of Elevai Skincare in January 2025, and the company expects to realize over $1 million in operational savings and synergies in fiscal year 2025.

The combination with 20/20 advances the company’s March 10, announcement of its new strategy to build a footprint in diagnostics to drive synergies with its existing bio-aesthetic portfolio of products and its contemplated expansion into nutrition. 

20/20 pre-merger stockholders are expected to own about 50.1% of the combined company, and Longevity (NASDAQ:XAGE) pre-merger stockholders are expected to own the rest.

Rajiv Shukla, Chairman and CEO of Longevity, will remain Chairman while Jonathan Cohen will be appointed CEO of the combined company.
